entry.title: Compensation Promotion with Position Management enabled
entry.content:
Hello Compensation experts (and @xavierlegarrec ), 

I have been wondering what the leading practice is/should be in terms of enabling the promotion tool in the Compensation module, especially when Position management is in place. 

From my understanding (I am not an EC/Position Management expert), it is usually accepted as a best practice that fields that are managed at the position level (like job classification, pay grade etc) should not be changed directly in the Job information portlet. 

However, when promoting an employee in compensation, selecting a new job classification or pay grade is often exactly what the compensation planner wants to do. So this is where i get confused: if we should not update fields in job info, then we cannot really publish the promotions recorded in compensation to the job info portlet. The only way we could publish them would be to have positions created in advance of the compensation planners recording the promotions, and the planners would then have to chose the right position for the employee to be promoted to. This seems very impractical. 

So far what i have done is to use the promotion tool in compensation to record the promotions suggested  by the planner, but these do not get published automatically and instead, and admin has to process the promotions manually/via imports by first creating the positions and then updating the job information to the correct new position. Is there a better way to do this according to you? 

Also, on a similar topic, in some instances, it is desirable for customers to have the possibility to change the pay grade or job classification of an employee without changing their positions. For instance a member of their team started 2 years ago as a Junior accountant, and should now be promoted to associate accountant, but without changing position/responsibilities (similar to the pay scale functionality). Is this possible with Position Management enabled?

Thanks a lot for your insights

entry.title: Re: CDS Annotation to validate values against value help
entry.content: Are there any plans to enhance the @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition.useForValidation (default: true) annotation so that it works with additionalbinding, as you would get in a foreign key relationship in SAPGui?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Objects: How to Properly Reset an Instance to It...
entry.content: Unlike standard classes, an instance of CL_GUI_ALV_GRID needs a free() method to explicitly release frontend resources

entry.title: Automate Extraction – Project Users Maintained as Cloud Foundry Org/Space Members
entry.content:
Introduction

While reviewing SAP BTP Cloud Foundry access, I ran into a common operational challenge: users who were no longer part of a project were still maintained as Cloud Foundry org or space members.

Checking this manually across multiple SAP BTP subaccounts is time-consuming. Each subaccount may have a Cloud Foundry org, multiple spaces, and different user role assignments. If the landscape spans multiple Cloud Foundry API endpoints, the effort increases further.

In this blog, I am sharing an automation approach I used to extract Cloud Foundry org and space membership details across SAP BTP subaccounts and consolidate the results into CSV reports for review.

Problem Statement

Cloud Foundry access in SAP BTP is maintained at different levels:

  • Organization level
  • Space level

Users may be assigned roles such as:

  • Org Manager
  • Org Auditor
  • Org User
  • Space Manager
  • Space Developer
  • Space Auditor
  • Space Supporter

When project teams change, some users may no longer need access. However, if project offboarding does not include Cloud Foundry org and space membership cleanup, stale access can remain.

Manually validating this from the SAP BTP cockpit requires going through each subaccount, org, and space one by one. In my case, this could take close to a full business day.

Tools Used

I used the following command-line tools:

  1. SAP BTP CLI  -The SAP BTP CLI is used for SAP BTP account administration, including working with global accounts, subaccounts, and environment instances.
  2. Cloud Foundry CLI - The Cloud Foundry CLI is used to interact with the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime, including orgs, spaces, applications, services, and users.
High-Level Approach
 
The automation was split into two steps.
 
Step 1: Identify Cloud Foundry API Endpoints - First, I used the SAP BTP CLI to list subaccounts and environment instances. Then, for each subaccount, lists environment instances of a subaccount, such as Cloud Foundry orgs or Kyma clusters.
 
From this output, I identified the Cloud Foundry API endpoints used in the global account. 
Example endpoints:
https://api.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com
https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com
https://api.cf.us20.hana.ondemand.com
https://api.cf.us21.hana.ondemand.com

Step 2: Extract Org and Space Members - After collecting the API endpoints, I used the Cloud Foundry CLI to log in to each endpoint and extract:

Organizations
Spaces
Org-level role assignments
Space-level role assignments
User details
Role details

The script loops through each endpoint, retrieves all orgs, then retrieves spaces and role assignments for each org.

Why Include Both Org and Space Roles?

Initially, I focused only on org members. However, that did not give the complete access picture.
A user may not have a high-privilege org role but may still be assigned as a Space Developer or Space Manager in one or more spaces. For access review purposes, both org-level and space-level assignments are important.

Conclusion

Automating Cloud Foundry org and space membership extraction helped reduce a repetitive manual review process from almost a full business day to a few minutes.
The consolidated CSV output provides a clear view of users maintained at org and space levels across multiple SAP BTP Cloud Foundry endpoints. This makes access review faster, more consistent, and easier to repeat.
For teams managing multiple SAP BTP subaccounts, this type of automation can be a useful starting point for improving visibility into Cloud Foundry access.

How to Run the Automation

This section explains the practical steps to run the automation and generate the final Cloud Foundry org/space membership report.


Prerequisites
Before running the scripts, ensure the following tools are installed and accessible from PowerShell:

  • SAP BTP CLI
  • Cloud Foundry CLI
  • PowerShell
  • Access to the SAP BTP global account/subaccounts
  • Access to Cloud Foundry endpoints through SSO

Validate the CLI installations:

btp --version
cf version

If btp or cf is not recognized, add the CLI installation folder to the system/user PATH or call the executable using the full path.

$env:Path += ";C:\BTP"

Step 1: Log in to SAP BTP CLI
Log in to the SAP BTP global account:

btp login

Confirm the current BTP target:

btp target

The target should point to the intended global account before continuing.

Step 2: Extract Cloud Foundry API Endpoints
Run the endpoint discovery script:

cd C:\BTP
.\get-all-cf-api-endpoints.ps1

This script identifies Cloud Foundry-enabled subaccounts and generates two output files:

C:\BTP\btp-cf-api-endpoints-by-subaccount.csv
C:\BTP\btp-cf-api-endpoints-unique.txt

The detailed CSV contains subaccount-level details, while the unique text file contains the distinct Cloud Foundry API endpoints.

Step 3: Update the Endpoint List in the Extraction Script(export-org-and-space-members-multiple-endpoints.ps1)
Open the org/space member extraction script:

notepad C:\BTP\export-org-and-space-members-multiple-endpoints.ps1

Update the $Endpoints array with the API endpoints identified in Step 2.

Example:

$Endpoints = @(
    "https://api.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com",
    "https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com",
    "https://api.cf.us20.hana.ondemand.com",
    "https://api.cf.us21.hana.ondemand.com"
)

Step 4: Run the Org and Space Membership Extraction
Run the script:

cd C:\BTP
.\export-org-and-space-members-multiple-endpoints.ps1

For each endpoint, the script will display a passcode URL similar to:

https://login.cf.us21.hana.ondemand.com/passcode

Open the displayed URL in a browser, copy the temporary SSO passcode, paste it into the PowerShell prompt, and press Enter.
The script repeats this process for each Cloud Foundry API endpoint.

Step 5: Review the Output File
After the script completes, it generates a consolidated CSV report:

C:\BTP\all-cf-endpoint-org-space-members.csv

============================================================================
get-all-cf-api-endpoints.ps1 Source Code
============================================================================

# ============================================================
# Get all Cloud Foundry API endpoints from all BTP subaccounts
# Output:
#   C:\BTP\btp-cf-api-endpoints-by-subaccount.csv
#   C:\BTP\btp-cf-api-endpoints-unique.txt
#
# Prerequisite:
#   Already logged in using: btp login
# ============================================================

$DetailOutFile = "C:\BTP\btp-cf-api-endpoints-by-subaccount.csv"
$UniqueOutFile = "C:\BTP\btp-cf-api-endpoints-unique.txt"

$Results = @()

function Get-JsonArray {
    param($JsonObject)

    if ($null -eq $JsonObject) {
        return @()
    }

    if ($JsonObject -is [System.Array]) {
        return @($JsonObject)
    }

    foreach ($prop in @("value", "items", "content", "subaccounts", "environmentInstances", "resources")) {
        if ($JsonObject.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains $prop) {
            return @($JsonObject.$prop)
        }
    }

    return @($JsonObject)
}

function Get-FirstPropertyValue {
    param(
        $Object,
        [string[]]$PropertyNames
    )

    if ($null -eq $Object) {
        return $null
    }

    foreach ($name in $PropertyNames) {
        if ($Object.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains $name) {
            $value = $Object.$name
            if ($null -ne $value -and "$value".Trim() -ne "") {
                return "$value"
            }
        }
    }

    return $null
}

function Get-CfApiEndpointFromObject {
    param(
        $EnvObject,
        [string]$SubaccountRegion
    )

    # 1. Try to find a full API endpoint directly in the JSON.
    $jsonText = $EnvObject | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 30 -Compress

    if ($jsonText -match "https://api\.cf\.[a-zA-Z0-9-]+\.hana\.ondemand\.com") {
        return $Matches[0]
    }

    # 2. Try to derive from landscape label or region.
    $candidateValues = @()

    $landscape = Get-FirstPropertyValue $EnvObject @(
        "landscapeLabel",
        "landscape",
        "landscape_label",
        "platformId",
        "platform_id",
        "region"
    )

    if ($landscape) {
        $candidateValues += $landscape
    }

    if ($SubaccountRegion) {
        $candidateValues += $SubaccountRegion
    }

    foreach ($candidate in $candidateValues) {
        if (-not $candidate) {
            continue
        }

        $value = "$candidate".Trim()

        # Examples:
        # cf-us21        -> us21
        # cf-us21-001    -> us21
        # us21           -> us21
        # us21-001       -> us21
        if ($value -match "^cf[-_](?<region>[a-z]{2}[0-9]{2})(?:[-_][0-9]+)?$") {
            return "https://api.cf.$($Matches.region).hana.ondemand.com"
        }

        if ($value -match "^(?<region>[a-z]{2}[0-9]{2})(?:[-_][0-9]+)?$") {
            return "https://api.cf.$($Matches.region).hana.ondemand.com"
        }
    }

    return $null
}

Write-Host "Getting subaccounts from current BTP global account..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

$subRaw = btp --format json list accounts/subaccount

if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
    Write-Host "Failed to list subaccounts. Confirm you are logged in with btp login." -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

$subJson = $subRaw | ConvertFrom-Json
$subaccounts = Get-JsonArray $subJson

Write-Host "Found $($subaccounts.Count) subaccount record(s)." -ForegroundColor Green

foreach ($sub in $subaccounts) {
    $subaccountId = Get-FirstPropertyValue $sub @(
        "guid",
        "id",
        "subaccountId",
        "subaccountGuid",
        "subaccountGUID",
        "subaccount id"
    )

    $subaccountName = Get-FirstPropertyValue $sub @(
        "displayName",
        "display name",
        "name"
    )

    $subdomain = Get-FirstPropertyValue $sub @(
        "subdomain"
    )

    $region = Get-FirstPropertyValue $sub @(
        "region"
    )

    if (-not $subaccountId) {
        Write-Host "Skipping subaccount record because no subaccount ID was found." -ForegroundColor Yellow
        continue
    }

    Write-Host "`nChecking subaccount: $subaccountName [$subaccountId]" -ForegroundColor Yellow

    try {
        $envRaw = btp --format json list accounts/environment-instance --subaccount $subaccountId

        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                SubaccountName = $subaccountName
                SubaccountId   = $subaccountId
                Subdomain      = $subdomain
                Region         = $region
                Environment    = ""
                EnvironmentId  = ""
                CfApiEndpoint  = ""
                Status         = "Error listing environment instances"
            }

            continue
        }

        $envJson = $envRaw | ConvertFrom-Json
        $envInstances = Get-JsonArray $envJson

        if (-not $envInstances -or $envInstances.Count -eq 0) {
            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                SubaccountName = $subaccountName
                SubaccountId   = $subaccountId
                Subdomain      = $subdomain
                Region         = $region
                Environment    = ""
                EnvironmentId  = ""
                CfApiEndpoint  = ""
                Status         = "No environment instances"
            }

            continue
        }

        foreach ($env in $envInstances) {
            $envText = ($env | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 30 -Compress)

            $isCloudFoundry =
                $envText -match "cloudfoundry" -or
                $envText -match "Cloud Foundry" -or
                $envText -match "cloud foundry"

            if (-not $isCloudFoundry) {
                continue
            }

            $envName = Get-FirstPropertyValue $env @(
                "name",
                "displayName",
                "environment",
                "environmentType",
                "serviceName",
                "service"
            )

            $envId = Get-FirstPropertyValue $env @(
                "id",
                "guid",
                "environmentInstanceId"
            )

            $endpoint = Get-CfApiEndpointFromObject -EnvObject $env -SubaccountRegion $region

            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                SubaccountName = $subaccountName
                SubaccountId   = $subaccountId
                Subdomain      = $subdomain
                Region         = $region
                Environment    = $envName
                EnvironmentId  = $envId
                CfApiEndpoint  = $endpoint
                Status         = if ($endpoint) { "Found" } else { "Cloud Foundry found, endpoint not derived" }
            }
        }
    }
    catch {
        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
            SubaccountName = $subaccountName
            SubaccountId   = $subaccountId
            Subdomain      = $subdomain
            Region         = $region
            Environment    = ""
            EnvironmentId  = ""
            CfApiEndpoint  = ""
            Status         = "Error: $($_.Exception.Message)"
        }
    }
}

$Results |
    Sort-Object SubaccountName, CfApiEndpoint |
    Export-Csv -Path $DetailOutFile -NoTypeInformation

$Results |
    Where-Object { $_.CfApiEndpoint -and $_.CfApiEndpoint.Trim() -ne "" } |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty CfApiEndpoint -Unique |
    Sort-Object |
    Set-Content -Path $UniqueOutFile

Write-Host "`nDone." -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Detailed output: $DetailOutFile" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Unique endpoints: $UniqueOutFile" -ForegroundColor Green

============================================================================
export-org-and-space-members-multiple-endpoints.ps1 Source Code
============================================================================

# ============================================================
# Export Cloud Foundry org + space members from multiple endpoints
# Output: C:\BTP\all-cf-endpoint-org-space-members.csv
#
# Safety:
#   READ-ONLY against Cloud Foundry.
#   Uses cf login and cf curl GET requests only.
# ============================================================

$OutFile = "C:\BTP\all-cf-endpoint-org-space-members.csv"

$Endpoints = @(
    "https://api.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com",
    "https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com",
    "https://api.cf.us20.hana.ondemand.com",
    "https://api.cf.us21.hana.ondemand.com"
)

$Results = @()

function Get-LoginHostFromApiEndpoint {
    param([string]$Endpoint)
    return ($Endpoint -replace "^https://api\.", "https://login.") + "/passcode"
}

function Invoke-CfGetPaged {
    param([string]$Path)

    $AllResources = @()
    $AllIncludedUsers = @()
    $NextPath = $Path

    while ($NextPath) {
        $Raw = cf curl $NextPath

        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
            throw "cf curl failed for path: $NextPath"
        }

        $Json = $Raw | ConvertFrom-Json

        if ($Json.resources) {
            $AllResources += $Json.resources
        }

        if ($Json.included -and $Json.included.users) {
            $AllIncludedUsers += $Json.included.users
        }

        if ($Json.pagination -and $Json.pagination.next -and $Json.pagination.next.href) {
            $NextHref = $Json.pagination.next.href

            if ($NextHref -like "http*") {
                $Uri = [System.Uri]$NextHref
                $NextPath = $Uri.PathAndQuery
            }
            else {
                $NextPath = $NextHref
            }
        }
        else {
            $NextPath = $null
        }
    }

    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        Resources = $AllResources
        Users     = $AllIncludedUsers
    }
}

function Convert-CfRoleName {
    param([string]$Role)

    switch ($Role) {
        "organization_manager"         { return "Org Manager" }
        "organization_auditor"         { return "Org Auditor" }
        "organization_user"            { return "Org User" }
        "organization_billing_manager" { return "Billing Manager" }
        "space_manager"                { return "Space Manager" }
        "space_developer"              { return "Space Developer" }
        "space_auditor"                { return "Space Auditor" }
        "space_supporter"              { return "Space Supporter" }
        default                        { return $Role }
    }
}

function Get-UserDetailsFromRoleResponse {
    param(
        $RoleResponse
    )

    $UsersByGuid = @{}

    foreach ($User in $RoleResponse.Users) {
        if ($User.guid -and -not $UsersByGuid.ContainsKey($User.guid)) {
            $UsersByGuid[$User.guid] = $User
        }
    }

    return $UsersByGuid
}

function Get-UserGuidFromRole {
    param($Role)

    if ($Role.relationships -and
        $Role.relationships.user -and
        $Role.relationships.user.data -and
        $Role.relationships.user.data.guid) {
        return $Role.relationships.user.data.guid
    }

    return $null
}

Write-Host "SCRIPT STARTED: Export org + space members from multiple CF endpoints" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Output file: $OutFile" -ForegroundColor Cyan

foreach ($Endpoint in $Endpoints) {
    Write-Host "`n============================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host "Processing endpoint: $Endpoint" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host "============================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan

    $PasscodeUrl = Get-LoginHostFromApiEndpoint -Endpoint $Endpoint

    Write-Host "`nOpen this URL in browser and copy a fresh SSO passcode:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host $PasscodeUrl -ForegroundColor White

    $Passcode = Read-Host "Enter SSO passcode for $Endpoint"

    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Passcode)) {
        Write-Host "No passcode entered. Skipping endpoint: $Endpoint" -ForegroundColor Red

        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Endpoint   = $Endpoint
            OrgName    = ""
            OrgGuid    = ""
            SpaceName  = ""
            SpaceGuid  = ""
            Email      = ""
            Origin     = ""
            OrgRoles   = ""
            SpaceRoles = ""
            Status     = "Skipped - no SSO passcode entered"
        }

        continue
    }

    Write-Host "Logging in to $Endpoint ..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
    cf login -a $Endpoint --sso-passcode $Passcode

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
        Write-Host "Login failed for endpoint: $Endpoint" -ForegroundColor Red

        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Endpoint   = $Endpoint
            OrgName    = ""
            OrgGuid    = ""
            SpaceName  = ""
            SpaceGuid  = ""
            Email      = ""
            Origin     = ""
            OrgRoles   = ""
            SpaceRoles = ""
            Status     = "Login failed"
        }

        continue
    }

    try {
        Write-Host "Getting organizations from $Endpoint ..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
        $OrgResponse = Invoke-CfGetPaged "/v3/organizations?per_page=5000"
        $Orgs = $OrgResponse.Resources

        if (-not $Orgs -or $Orgs.Count -eq 0) {
            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                OrgName    = ""
                OrgGuid    = ""
                SpaceName  = ""
                SpaceGuid  = ""
                Email      = ""
                Origin     = ""
                OrgRoles   = ""
                SpaceRoles = ""
                Status     = "No orgs found or no org access"
            }

            continue
        }

        foreach ($Org in $Orgs) {
            $OrgName = $Org.name
            $OrgGuid = $Org.guid

            Write-Host "`nProcessing org: $OrgName" -ForegroundColor Yellow

            # ------------------------------------------------------------
            # 1. Get org-level members and roles
            # ------------------------------------------------------------

            $OrgUserRoleMap = @{}
            $OrgUserOriginMap = @{}

            try {
                $OrgRoleResponse = Invoke-CfGetPaged "/v3/roles?organization_guids=$OrgGuid&include=user&per_page=5000"
                $OrgRoles = $OrgRoleResponse.Resources
                $OrgUsersByGuid = Get-UserDetailsFromRoleResponse -RoleResponse $OrgRoleResponse

                foreach ($Role in $OrgRoles) {
                    if ($Role.type -notlike "organization_*") {
                        continue
                    }

                    $UserGuid = Get-UserGuidFromRole -Role $Role

                    if (-not $UserGuid) {
                        continue
                    }

                    $UserObj = $OrgUsersByGuid[$UserGuid]

                    if ($UserObj) {
                        $Email = $UserObj.username
                        $Origin = $UserObj.origin
                    }
                    else {
                        $Email = $UserGuid
                        $Origin = "User details not included"
                    }

                    if (-not $OrgUserRoleMap.ContainsKey($Email)) {
                        $OrgUserRoleMap[$Email] = @()
                    }

                    $OrgUserRoleMap[$Email] += Convert-CfRoleName $Role.type
                    $OrgUserOriginMap[$Email] = $Origin
                }
            }
            catch {
                $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                    Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                    OrgName    = $OrgName
                    OrgGuid    = $OrgGuid
                    SpaceName  = ""
                    SpaceGuid  = ""
                    Email      = ""
                    Origin     = ""
                    OrgRoles   = ""
                    SpaceRoles = ""
                    Status     = "Error getting org roles: $($_.Exception.Message)"
                }
            }

            # Add org-level rows where no specific space is attached
            foreach ($Email in $OrgUserRoleMap.Keys) {
                $OrgRolesText = ($OrgUserRoleMap[$Email] | Sort-Object -Unique) -join ", "

                $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                    Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                    OrgName    = $OrgName
                    OrgGuid    = $OrgGuid
                    SpaceName  = ""
                    SpaceGuid  = ""
                    Email      = $Email
                    Origin     = $OrgUserOriginMap[$Email]
                    OrgRoles   = $OrgRolesText
                    SpaceRoles = ""
                    Status     = "Org-level member"
                }
            }

            # ------------------------------------------------------------
            # 2. Get spaces under this org
            # ------------------------------------------------------------

            try {
                $SpacesResponse = Invoke-CfGetPaged "/v3/spaces?organization_guids=$OrgGuid&per_page=5000"
                $Spaces = $SpacesResponse.Resources

                if (-not $Spaces -or $Spaces.Count -eq 0) {
                    continue
                }

                foreach ($Space in $Spaces) {
                    $SpaceName = $Space.name
                    $SpaceGuid = $Space.guid

                    Write-Host "Processing space members: $OrgName / $SpaceName" -ForegroundColor Gray

                    try {
                        $SpaceRoleResponse = Invoke-CfGetPaged "/v3/roles?space_guids=$SpaceGuid&include=user&per_page=5000"
                        $SpaceRoles = $SpaceRoleResponse.Resources
                        $SpaceUsersByGuid = Get-UserDetailsFromRoleResponse -RoleResponse $SpaceRoleResponse

                        if (-not $SpaceRoles -or $SpaceRoles.Count -eq 0) {
                            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                                Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                                OrgName    = $OrgName
                                OrgGuid    = $OrgGuid
                                SpaceName  = $SpaceName
                                SpaceGuid  = $SpaceGuid
                                Email      = ""
                                Origin     = ""
                                OrgRoles   = ""
                                SpaceRoles = ""
                                Status     = "No space members found"
                            }

                            continue
                        }

                        $SpaceUserRoleMap = @{}
                        $SpaceUserOriginMap = @{}

                        foreach ($Role in $SpaceRoles) {
                            if ($Role.type -notlike "space_*") {
                                continue
                            }

                            $UserGuid = Get-UserGuidFromRole -Role $Role

                            if (-not $UserGuid) {
                                continue
                            }

                            $UserObj = $SpaceUsersByGuid[$UserGuid]

                            if ($UserObj) {
                                $Email = $UserObj.username
                                $Origin = $UserObj.origin
                            }
                            else {
                                $Email = $UserGuid
                                $Origin = "User details not included"
                            }

                            if (-not $SpaceUserRoleMap.ContainsKey($Email)) {
                                $SpaceUserRoleMap[$Email] = @()
                            }

                            $SpaceUserRoleMap[$Email] += Convert-CfRoleName $Role.type
                            $SpaceUserOriginMap[$Email] = $Origin
                        }

                        foreach ($Email in $SpaceUserRoleMap.Keys) {
                            $SpaceRolesText = ($SpaceUserRoleMap[$Email] | Sort-Object -Unique) -join ", "

                            $OrgRolesText = ""
                            if ($OrgUserRoleMap.ContainsKey($Email)) {
                                $OrgRolesText = ($OrgUserRoleMap[$Email] | Sort-Object -Unique) -join ", "
                            }

                            $Origin = $SpaceUserOriginMap[$Email]
                            if (-not $Origin -and $OrgUserOriginMap.ContainsKey($Email)) {
                                $Origin = $OrgUserOriginMap[$Email]
                            }

                            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                                Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                                OrgName    = $OrgName
                                OrgGuid    = $OrgGuid
                                SpaceName  = $SpaceName
                                SpaceGuid  = $SpaceGuid
                                Email      = $Email
                                Origin     = $Origin
                                OrgRoles   = $OrgRolesText
                                SpaceRoles = $SpaceRolesText
                                Status     = "Space-level member"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                    catch {
                        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                            Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                            OrgName    = $OrgName
                            OrgGuid    = $OrgGuid
                            SpaceName  = $SpaceName
                            SpaceGuid  = $SpaceGuid
                            Email      = ""
                            Origin     = ""
                            OrgRoles   = ""
                            SpaceRoles = ""
                            Status     = "Error getting space members: $($_.Exception.Message)"
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            catch {
                $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                    Endpoint   = $Endpoint
                    OrgName    = $OrgName
                    OrgGuid    = $OrgGuid
                    SpaceName  = ""
                    SpaceGuid  = ""
                    Email      = ""
                    Origin     = ""
                    OrgRoles   = ""
                    SpaceRoles = ""
                    Status     = "Error getting spaces: $($_.Exception.Message)"
                }
            }
        }
    }
    catch {
        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Endpoint   = $Endpoint
            OrgName    = ""
            OrgGuid    = ""
            SpaceName  = ""
            SpaceGuid  = ""
            Email      = ""
            Origin     = ""
            OrgRoles   = ""
            SpaceRoles = ""
            Status     = "Error getting orgs: $($_.Exception.Message)"
        }
    }
}

$Results |
    Sort-Object Endpoint, OrgName, SpaceName, Email |
    Export-Csv -Path $OutFile -NoTypeInformation

Write-Host "`nExport completed." -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "File created: $OutFile" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Open with:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "notepad $OutFile" -ForegroundColor White

entry.title: Re: ABAP Objects: How to Properly Reset an Instance to Its Initial State
entry.content: Hi,Thank you for your feedback. You are absolutely right—FREE is not a universal method in ABAP.In my post, I was specifically referring to CL_GUI_ALV_GRID (and its associated containers). Since these handle frontend resources via the Control Framework, the free() method is crucial to prevent memory leaks, which is an exception to the standard Garbage Collection logic.Also, I appreciate your reminder about the Q&A format. I will make sure to post questions and answers as distinct objects in the future to keep the community organized.Best regards

entry.title: Starting a Career in SAP Consulting as a Recent Graduate
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I recently graduated in Management Information Systems and I am very interested in starting a career in the SAP ecosystem, especially in ERP and HR-related solutions such as SAP HCM and SuccessFactors.

During my internships in manufacturing and retail companies, I gained some experience with ERP systems, business processes, data management, and SQL. I was also exposed to systems like IFS ERP and SAP, which increased my interest in this field.

At the moment, I am trying to improve myself by exploring SAP Community, basic SAP courses, and LinkedIn Learning content. I enjoy learning about how SAP solutions help companies manage their processes more efficiently.

As someone at the beginning of this journey, I would really appreciate your advice:

  • How did you start your SAP consulting career?
  • Which areas or modules would you recommend for a beginner?
  • What skills should I focus on improving first?
  • Are there any resources, certifications, or projects you would suggest?

Thank you in advance for your guidance and recommendations!

entry.title: Clarification on Transport Management for Company Codes and Organizational Structure in SAP S/4HANA

We would like to inquire whether there is a possibility to deactivate, delete, or exclude Company Codes from being transported to other environments.

We have already confirmed our organizational structure, and we would like to avoid transporting the existing Company Codes that are currently incorrect.

In case we create new legal entities / Company Codes, would it be possible to transport only the newly created entities without impacting or transporting the old incorrect Company Codes?

Could you please advise on the recommended approach and feasibility in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Best regards,

entry.title: Accelerating SAP Fiori & CAP Development With SAP MCP Server + GitHub Copilot in VS Code
entry.content:
☕The 5-Hour CAP App That Now Takes 30 Minutes

Last month, I sat down with one of our experienced SAP CAP developers and asked her to build a small Fiori Elements app on top of a CAP Java service — start to first working screen. She'd done dozens of these. It took her just under five hours.

A week later, I asked a colleague — a senior ABAP developer who'd never written a line of CDS or Spring Boot — to build the same thing using GitHub Copilot Chat plus the new SAP MCP Server in VS Code.

He shipped a working app — CAP service with one entity, Fiori Elements List Report + Object Page, draft handling, basic security — in 38 minutes

That gap isn't a fluke. It's the new reality for SAP development — and in this post I want to walk you through what changed, how the pieces fit together, and how to set this up yourself.

🎯Why This Matters Right Now

If you build on SAP, you've felt the friction:

  • 🐌Annotations are unforgiving. A single misplaced UI.LineItem token can swallow an afternoon.
  • 📚Documentation is fragmented across cap.cloud.sap, help.sap.com, blogs, and Discovery Center.
  • ☕CAP Java handlers require Spring Boot fluency that ABAP developers don't have on day one.
  • 🔌BTP wiring — mta.yaml, xs-security.json, destinations, role collections — is unforgiving and project-after-project repetitive.

That's exactly what changed in the last few months.

🧩 The Three Pieces That Snap Together

The breakthrough isn't any one tool — it's three components working as a system.

1️⃣Visual Studio Code

The neutral home base. Every SAP toolchain — CAP, Fiori Tools, Cloud MTA Build Tool, the BTP CLI — already lives comfortably here.

2️⃣GitHub Copilot Chat (Enterprise)

Not "Copilot suggests an autocomplete." This is the conversational, context-aware Copilot that can read your workspace, plan multi-step changes, and call external tools.

3️⃣SAP's MCP Servers (Three of Them)

This is the new piece. MCP — Model Context Protocol — is an open standard that lets AI assistants invoke external tools through a standard interface.

 

When Copilot Chat in Agent mode calls these servers, you get something neither tool delivers alone: an AI that understands CAP- and Fiori-specific patterns rather than producing generic "make me a Java app" output.

🔑 The mental model: GitHub Copilot is the brain. SAP MCP servers give it SAP-shaped hands.

📜 But Wait — Is This Actually Allowed Under the SAP API Policy?

It's the first question every architect and CoE lead asks the moment MCP enters the conversation — and rightly so. The SAP API Policy (published in the SAP Help Portal, latest FAQ v1.1, May 2026) sets clear guardrails for AI- and agent-driven access to SAP APIs.

So let's address it directly: yes, the three MCP servers used here are explicitly endorsed by SAP.

🟢 The Direct Quote

From Question 37 of the SAP API Policy FAQ:

"SAP-managed and SAP-published MCP servers (for example, the CAP MCP Server, UI5 MCP Server, and Fiori Elements MCP Server for developer tooling) are themselves endorsed. MCP Gateway on SAP Integration Suite … is also an endorsed path for managed MCP access to SAP endpoints subject to the compliance with the API Policy…"

🛠 Setting Up the Stack (≈ 15 minutes)

Step 1 — Prerequisites Check

Before we configure MCP, verify:

  1. node -v :: Should be v20.x or higher
  2. npm -v :: Should be 10.x or higher
  3. npx --version :: Should work

Step 2 — Install VS Code Extensions

  • SAP CDS Language Support
  • SAP Fiori Tools – Extension Pack
  • GitHub Copilot + GitHub Copilot Chat
  • Extension Pack for Java 

Step 3 — Create Your Workspace and MCP Configuration

In your workspace, create .vscode/mcp.json:

Paste this exact content:

​

Step 4 — Start the MCP Servers

​Open Command Palette: Ctrl + Shift + P

​Type: MCP: List Servers → press Enter

​You'll see all servers listed here on right hand side.

​For each server, click → Start Server

​​Step 5 — Enable Agent Mode in Copilot

​Open Copilot Chat (Ctrl + Alt + I)

​Bottom of chat input → mode dropdown → switch to Agent

​Click 🛠tools icon →  cap, fiori, ui5 tools appear

That's it. You're ready.

Your First "Wow" Prompt

Open Copilot Chat in Agent mode and try this:

"Use the cap MCP server to initialize a new CAP Java project named scheduling-agreements with the SQLite profile, namespace com.example.scheduling, and configure mock users admin/admin and viewer/viewer in application.yaml."

In about 20 seconds you'll have a fully wired project: db/, srv/, app/, a Maven pom.xml, an application.yaml with mock users, and a .gitignore that won't betray your local SQLite database into source control.

Now follow up:

"Create entity SchedulingAgreement with fields ID (UUID, key), vendor (String 50, mandatory), plant (String 4), validFrom (Date), validTo (Date), totalValue (Decimal 15,2) with Currency. Include the managed aspect."

You'll get production-quality CDS — properly typed, annotated with @title/@description, currency association from @sap/cds/common, all the small craftsmanship details an experienced CAP dev would apply.

Then:

"Define a service exposing this entity, and use the fiori MCP server to scaffold a Fiori Elements List Report + Object Page module with i18n in en, de, es, pt-BR."

About 90 seconds later, you can run mvn spring-boot:run, open the Fiori preview, and see a working app.

entry.title: CK11N not picking updated Purchasing Info Record price unless PO exists
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am running into an issue with CK11N (standard cost estimate) and price determination from purchasing.

Situation:

I am a functional consultant working for a company where we apply surcharges on purchasing prices. We want CK11N to determine the material price based on the Purchasing Info Record (including conditions/surcharges).

Configuration:

In the Valuation Variant, I have maintained the following:

  • Strategy sequence:

    1. Price from Purchasing Info Record
    2. Valuation price according to price control
    3. Planned price 1

  • Sub-strategy (Purchasing Info Record valuation):

    • Net Purchase Order Price (also tested with other options)
    • Purchase Order Price via Condition Table 
    • Gross Quotation Price

Problem:

When I:

  1. Update the price in the Purchasing Info Record (ME12)
  2. Run CK11N

The price is NOT updated in the cost estimate

However:

  1. If I create a Purchase Order (ME21N)
  2. Then run CK11N again

The price is updated correctly

 

Question:

  • Why does CK11N seem to ignore the updated info record unless a PO exists?
  • Which sub-strategy should be used to ensure CK11N reads info record conditions directly?
  • Is there additional customizing required for condition-based valuation to work without a PO?

entry.title: SAP GROW Fast: Швидкий старт для компаній у сфері професійних послуг
entry.content:
Компанії у сфері професійних послуг часто стикаються з однією і тією ж проблемою: розрізнені системи, ручне зведення даних між фінансами, проєктами та операціями — і жодної єдиної картини бізнесу.

Що таке SAP GROW Fast?

SAP GROW Fast — це повноцінний сервіс впровадження, який забезпечує перехід на SAP Cloud ERP і виводить компанію на повну операційну готовність за кілька тижнів з фіксованою вартістю та передбачуваним результатом. Рішення створено для консалтингових фірм, IT-компаній, агентств та інших організацій, де в центрі бізнесу — люди, проєкти та клієнти.

Рішення охоплює готові PSA-процеси (Professional Services Automation) — від першого етапу проєкту до виставлення рахунку: планування проєктів, управління ресурсами, фіксація часу та витрат, виставлення рахунків, фінансова аналітика в реальному часі та AI-аналітика. Все це — на єдиній платформі, з наскрізною прозорістю від старту проєкту до отримання оплати та повною локалізацією для України: відповідність законодавству, інтеграція з КЕП та україномовний інтерфейс.

Реальна історія успіху: Business Evolution

Business Evolution — українська IT-консалтингова компанія, яка здійснила перехід з 1С на SAP Cloud ERP за 90 днів і отримала єдину платформу для повноцінного управління клієнтськими проєктами. Цифри говорять самі за себе:

  • 60% скорочення часу фінансового закриття — до 4 днів
  • 80% автоматизація опрацювання клієнтських рахунків
  • 122 клієнтські проєкти об’єднані в одній фінансовій моделі
  • 30% скорочення витрат на персонал

🏆Проєкт здобув перемогу в регіоні CEE у SAP Quality Awards 2025 у категорії «Rapid Time to Value» — за швидкий запуск, високу якість реалізації та реальний бізнес-ефект.

Детальна інформація про SAP GROW Fast, повний опис рішення та умови — на лендінг-сторінці SAP GROW Fast.

Хочете дізнатись більше?

Щоб побачити рішення в дії та почути, як колеги по індустрії вже вирішують ці виклики — приєднуйтесь до вебінару! 
SAP Professional Services Webinar - 2 червня, 16:00–17:30 за українським часом.
Програма охопить ключові теми: огляд трендів індустрії, End-to-End ProServ процеси з SAP-рішеннями, AI у сфері професійних послуг — від теорії до практичної цінності, живе демо системи в дії, клієнтські кейси та детальний огляд SAP GROW Fast. Захід дасть вам можливість отримати практичні інсайти та визначити наступні кроки для зростання.

Не пропустіть — реєструйтесь за посиланням та додавайте до календаря! 

💬Поділіться цим постом з колегами у сфері професійних послуг — можливо, саме вони шукають відповідь на ці питання прямо зараз.

entry.title: Re: ME51N Document Number at Creation
entry.content:
If you mean that after saving, the confirmation message including the created document number doesn't appear, this is probably caused by some tab merging in your flavor. This goes through all the tabs before displaying the screen, and during that process, a success message will disappear. You can try reproducing this manually without a flavor, when you click through tabs after receiving the confirmation message.

To show the document number, you can use some script that grabs it right after saving and then display it in the status bar or a popup.

If this is not the case, then there may be some other scripting action in the flavor, causing the issue. Difficult to say without taking a look at the actual scenario in your system.

Also, you need to upgrade from that old SP16, since it's out of maintenance/support.

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management enabled
entry.content:
Hi @catmoye 

Anything is fine except the position itself.

In regards to fields changed by planners in the promotion popup that could become out of sync with the original position defaults values I'm afraid there is no perfect world here and the only solution would be to use a lookup table to prevent these employees from publishing back to EC until they've been checked by an administrator.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from a Custom SAP Fiori Application - My experiences
entry.content:
This blog is to share my experience with integrating SAP UI5 task into SBPA(SAP Build Process Automation ) best way to start integration of SAP UI5 task into Build Process Automation .

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-sapui5-task-orderapproval.html

SAP uses predefined templating to create these workflow task as per above learning  journey . 

There is blog post which says one use custom fiori application to trigger . i tried this approach  first . my observations are mostly around this . 

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/triggering-sap-build-process-automation-workflow-from-a-custom-sap-fiori/m-p/13941417#:~:text=Click%20on%20configure%20button%20and%20add%20input.&text=Now%20our%20api%20trigger%20is,be%20created%20in%20BTP%20cockpit.&text=Ensure%20the%20destination%20is%20correctly,inputs%20required%20for%20your%20workflow .

This allows me to trigger Build Process Workflow but this configuration doesn't allow me to consider current user context also these custom fiori apps cant used in Build Process Automation as UI5 Tasks  . 

Observed a difference how URL is generated when these applications  are deployed to HTML5 repository . Apps which uses workflow templates are MTA Based & Custom fiori application used Manage App Router  , because of these custom fiori apps cant integrated as UI5 tasks in SBPA.  

URL for Workflow based 

URL for Custom Fiori App 

 .

even with Custom fiori application i am not able create workflow when i debug i found that SBPA needs a IRPA api key while making call to API . 

how  to get API key is explained  below .  

1) Navigate to Build Process Control Tower . 

2) Go to Environment where are workflows Deployed '

3). API Keys and add API Key , select the scope and create the key at the end of the you will have KEY which can be used in the UI5 application .

entry.title: Re: Prefer:odata.continue-on-error - Not working in ODATA...
entry.content:
The odata.continue-on-error depends on the backend implementation - it's not related with the IDE you are working on.

But giving the fact that you are using jQuery to call the OData API using a $batch call, you might want to review that decision. It's indeed helpful to show here the payload, in this case including headers.

Also, it's a must to provide further details on how this is implemented in the backend. I understood that you were not able to use the odata.continue-on-error in Postman as well but only in the individual requests.

entry.title: Your AI Agents Are Reshaping Your Workforce. Is Anyone Governing That?
entry.content:
Most AI agent governance conversations focus on IT risk and compliance. Almost none address what agents mean for the people whose work they're changing. Ask most organizations what they're doing to govern their AI agents, and you'll hear about registries, compliance mappings, and access controls. These matter. But they answer questions about systems, not people.

The question that is rarely asked is this: as AI agents take on more of the work your employees currently do, which roles are changing, which skills are becoming less relevant, and where does your organization need to invest in reskilling before the gap becomes a crisis?

AI agents have moved well beyond automating isolated tasks. They now orchestrate workflows, make decisions within defined parameters, and interact with multiple systems in sequence. The workforce implications are no longer hypothetical. They are already arriving, and in most organizations, nobody in a formal governance role is watching.

This is a significant gap, and one that AI governance platforms have almost entirely ignored.

The dominant framing for agent governance has been technical: which agents exist, who owns them, are they compliant, are they performing. These are the right questions for IT governance teams and enterprise architects, and they need to be answered. But they are not the only questions that matter to the organization. HR leaders, workforce planners, and business unit heads are facing a different set of concerns. How is our workforce changing as agents are deployed? Which teams are most affected? Are we investing in the right skills for the environment we're building toward?

Without visibility into the connection between agent deployments and workforce impact, organizations are making significant decisions about their people without the data to support them.

The SAP AI Agent Hub addresses this directly. By connecting agent governance to workforce planning, the platform enables organizations to map each deployed agent to the roles and skills it affects. This gives HR and business leaders a dynamic view of how agent deployments are reshaping workforce needs: which jobs are evolving, which capabilities are in growing demand, where reskilling investment is most urgently needed. These questions become answerable when agent governance is grounded in the people context, not just the IT context.

This is a meaningful differentiator. Connecting agent governance to the IT landscape, business processes, and workforce impact together, in a single platform, is something no other governance approach currently delivers in full. Workforce-focused platforms address the HR dimension well but lack architecture and process context. IT-focused platforms govern agents rigorously but leave the human impact invisible. The SAP AI Agent Hub is designed to close that gap.

Enterprise architects have a role to play here too. EAs already understand system interdependencies at the enterprise level. They know which applications connect to which processes, and which parts of the technology landscape are most sensitive to change. Applying that same lens to the boundary between agents and people is a natural extension of the role. As agents become structural components of how work gets done, the architecture model needs to account for workforce context alongside application and process context.

The organizations that will manage this transition well are not the ones that react when workforce disruption is already visible, when attrition spikes, when skill gaps become critical, when the human cost of ungoverned AI deployment lands on the CHRO's desk. They are the ones who saw it coming in the governance data, because they built a governance model that included their people from the start.

AI agent governance that only asks "what do we have and is it compliant?" is answering half the question. The other half is: what is it doing to our organization, and are we ready for that?

entry.title: Enable Payment Approval Process Before Posting Payment Entry in SAP
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need an approval process to be implemented before the payment entry is posted in SAP. The process should ensure that:

  • Payment entries are submitted for approval before posting.
  • Only authorized approvers can approve/reject the payment.
  • Posting should happen only after approval is completed.
  • Approval trail/history should be maintained for audit purposes.
  • Notification or workflow trigger should be available for approvers.

    Please review and provide the feasible solution/configuration for implementing this workflow in SAP.

 Regards,
Nandini

entry.title: Introducing SAP AI Agent Hub: Your command center for enterprise-grade AI governance
entry.content:
As AI agents proliferate across platforms and vendors, organizations increasingly lack visibility into which agents exist, what they do, and whether they operate safely. SAP AI Agent Hub is a vendor-agnostic AI governance solution that addresses this directly: it gives enterprise architects, IT administrators, and AI Centers of Excellence a single, structured system of record for all AI assets in the organization, regardless of where they were built or which platform they run on. It covers AI agents, large language models (LLMs), and MCP servers across your entire IT landscape. 

SAP AI Agent Hub is available for Business AI Platform customers who get Joule Studio and/ or AI Units, and as part of SAP LeanIX, SAP's enterprise architecture management solution. 

Discover and inventory your AI assets 

SAP AI Agent Hub automatically discovers AI agents, LLMs, and MCP servers from the platforms where they are built and deployed. Supported sources include Microsoft Entra ID, Google Agent Engine, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Azure API Center, and, new with this release, ServiceNow and SAP AI Core. All discovered assets are organized in a centralized inventory with classification, status, and architecture context. 

Evaluate and verify AI assets 

Once discovered, AI agents and MCP servers enter your inventory in an unverified state. Structured assessments capture key governance attributes for each asset, including AI risk rating, build type, and compliance mappings. Architecture decisions can be attached directly to each fact sheet, giving you a place to document the rationale behind agent adoption, platform choices, and compliance boundaries — and informing whether an asset should be verified or not. Based on this assessment, you can mark any asset as verified, rejected, or unverified, with a verification badge appearing on each fact sheet. Going forward, the badge will be picked up by the Joule Studio runtime to inform which agents are approved to run in production, and through an integration with SAP Integration Suite, it will indicate which MCP servers are verified for use by your developers. 

Outlook 

We are planning to further enhance SAP AI Agent Hub for it to become the central command center of AI assets, surfacing relevant information from diverse sources and ensuring a seamless user journey to access relevant data: 

  • Observability will give you visibility into the runtime behavior, performance, and usage of your AI agents and models in production, with drill-down into individual sessions via SAP Cloud ALM. 
  • Verification status of agents and MCP servers will be visible in Joule Studio and SAP Integration Suite and indicate safe usage.  
  • Identity and access control, built on SAP Cloud Identity Services, will enable you to centrally manage access, identities, and policies for AI agents across your organization. 
  • Agent mining, via an integration with SAP Signavio, will help you understand how agents execute tasks across defined process steps, measuring conformance and coverage. 
  • Org chart and skills mapping via SAP SuccessFactors will allow you to map AI agent deployment to business units and organizational structures, helping you understand workforce impact as agent adoption scales. 

With this, we continue to bring more visibility to your AI ecosystem to give you the confidence to scale your AI strategies.

entry.title: Embedding Custom Service List Views in Application UI
entry.content:
This feature enables administrators to embed custom service list views within both standard and custom UIs via adaptation configuration, with support for passing contextual parameters from the host UI.

Business Benefits:

By embedding relevant custom service lists views directly in the application UI   where users need them, employees spend less time navigating between screens and more time focusing on value-added tasks. Also ensures consistency with the native look and feel of the application.

Passing parameters from the host UI enables users to see contextually filtered or relevant records automatically.

Example Scenario

Consider a work order management system where each work order is linked to an employee. An employee needs quick access to view all work orders assigned to them without navigating away from their current workspace.

Solution: Embed the work order list view directly in the employee's details, automatically filtered by their employee ID to display only their assigned work orders.

Please Note : This blog focuses exclusively on embedding the list view component —not on building the underlying work order example service itself.

How to do:

  • Metadata JSON

The work order-to-employee(project Lead) relationship is defined in the service metadata JSON configuration, establishing the association between work orders and their assigned employees.

{

                    "name": "projectLead",

                    "dataType": "OBJECT",

                    "creatable": true,

                    "updatable": true,

                    "nullable": true,

                    "sortable": false,

                    "filterable": true,

                    "searchable": false,

                    "label": "Project Lead",

                    "objectDefinition": [

                        {

                            "name": "id",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": true,

                            "updatable": true,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": false,

                            "filterable": true,

                            "searchable": false,

                            "label": "Project Lead ID",

                            "dataFormat": "UUID",

                            "descriptionAttribute": "formattedName",

                            "keyType": "FOREIGN",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "targetAttribute": "id",

                                "targetEntity": "sap.crm.md.employeeservice.entity.employee",

                                "keyGroup": "projectLead",

                                "targetService": "sap.crm.md.service.employeeService",

                                "sourceEntity": "customer.ssc.workorderservice.entity.workOrder",

                                "associationType": "ASSOCIATION",

                                "sourceAttribute": "projectLead.id"

                            },

                            "analyticsRelevant": true,

                            "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead_id",

                            "searchWeightage": 1

                        },

                        {

                            "name": "displayId",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": false,

                            "updatable": false,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": true,

                            "filterable": false,

                            "searchable": true,

                            "displayId": true,

                            "label": "Employee Display ID",

                            "dataFormat": "ALPHANUMERIC",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "associationType": "REFERENCE",

                                "targetAttribute": "displayId",

                                "keyGroup": "projectLead",

                                "sourceEntity": "customer.ssc.workorderservice.entity.workOrder"

                            },

                            "analyticsRelevant": true,

                            "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead_displayId",

                            "searchWeightage": 1

                        },

                        {

                            "name": "formattedName",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": false,

                            "updatable": false,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": true,

                            "filterable": false,

                            "searchable": true,

                            "label": "Employee Name",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "associationType": "REFERENCE",

                                "targetAttribute": "formattedName",

                                "keyGroup": "projectLead",

                                "sourceEntity": "customer.ssc.workorderservice.entity.workOrder"

                            },

                            "analyticsRelevant": true,

                            "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead_formattedName",

                            "searchWeightage": 1

                        }

                    ],

                    "analyticsRelevant": true,

                    "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead",

                    "searchWeightage": 1

                },

To enable filtering by employee, then as shown above in metadata json for the projectLead(employee) ID field must be configured with the filterable property set to true. This marks the field as a valid filter criterion.

In external service handle the filter logic based on employee ID.

  •  Design App configuration

In the design App, add List Card available under Embedded Component section.

 

Select the filter parameters you want to apply to the embedded view.

A dialog will display all available filterable fields from your service metadata—these are fields marked with filterable: true.

For this example, select the Project Lead ID field (which represents the project lead or assigned employee) as your binding parameter.

Confirm and save your design changes to the application. The embedded component now knows which parameters to filter by.

  • Add Embedded component

Open the desired UI application where you want to embed the work order list view .Add a new section to the UI layout. Click Add Embedded Component.

A list displays all custom services that have embedded component (EC) added in their respective design apps.

Select the work order custom service from this list, then add its list view component to the section.

Click on bind parameters icon and bind the employee ID. This step is similar to mashup parameter binding.

Click on apply. Exit adaptation mode.  The embedded list view is displayed on UI as shown below.

              Employee ID is sent as Filter Parameter in Request :

 

When a filter parameter is passed from the UI to the embedded component, the external service must intercept and process this request(get request with filter parameter). The external service returns the filtered dataset back which is displayed in the embedded component on the UI. Filter parameters are optional and can be configured as per use case.

Note: Since the embedded component feature is available for custom UIs, in this example EC is added in workorder custom UI for demonstration. .If the embedded component feature is not available for your desired standard UI application, please raise a feature request against the specific application component.

Help Document

entry.title: Talent Intelligence Hub Certificates: Your Top Questions Answered
entry.content:
The new Certificates feature in Talent Intelligence Hub, which is part of the 1H 2026 release, enables organizations to manage certifications within the Growth Portfolio experience. Employees can add and maintain certifications relevant to their roles, while administrators can configure certificate types, validity periods, reminders, and workflows. The feature is designed to support ongoing credential management, improve visibility of employee certifications, and provide a foundation for future integrations and reporting enhancements.

General Setup & Configuration

1. How are certificates stored and managed?

Certificates are created as standard attributes in the Attributes Library and then mapped to employees. Administrators must first configure certificate records in the library.

2. Can users add certificates to their Growth Portfolio themselves?

Yes, provided the certificate already exists in the Attributes Library.

Notifications & Expiry Management

1. How is the expiration date derived?

The expiration date is calculated based on the certificate’s issue date and the validity period configured in the Attributes Library.

2. Are expiry reminder settings global or configurable per certificate?

Current preview setup uses global reminder settings. Future enhancements are expected to allow certificate-level overrides.

3. Can multiple reminder notifications be configured (for example 90, 30, and 7 days)?

Notification timing can be configured, and frequency settings such as daily, weekly or monthly are also available.

4. If a certificate expires, is the attached document automatically deleted?

No. Documents remain stored unless manually removed or overwritten.

Skills, Roles & Growth Portfolio

1. Are skills automatically added when a certificate is earned?

 Yes, Skills mapped to the certificate are added (provided it respects the target criteria) to Employee’s growth portfolio when certificate is added .

2. Can certificates be mass uploaded to employees’ Growth Portfolios?

Planned for future enhancement.

3. Is there a tool for bulk updating certificate data?

Planned for future release.

4. Can certificates be mapped to roles?

Planned for 2H2026 release.

5. Can a certificate be made mandatory for a role?

Planned for 2H2026 release.

6. Is it mandatory to associate a skill with a certificate?

No. Associating a skill with a certificate is optional. Certificates can be created and managed independently without requiring a linked skill.

Storage & Attachments

1. Where are uploaded certificate documents stored?

Documents are stored in the Document Management Service.

2. Is there a storage limit for certificate uploads?

A per-file size limit of 2 MB is allowed now. Overall storage follows tenant licensing/storage settings.

Advanced Scenarios

1. Can an administrator remove an outdated lower-level certification when a higher-level one is earned?

Yes, certificates can be removed from an employee’s Growth Portfolio.

2. Is there support for certification equivalency or higher-level progression?

Under evaluation.

3. What happens if a skill linked to a certificate normally requires workflow approval? Will it be added automatically or go through approval?

If the skill is added through a certificate association, it will be added automatically and will not trigger a workflow approval. Currently, workflow approvals are triggered only when a user directly requests a skill rating change through the UI.

Existing Systems & Migration

1. How does this Certificates feature relate to existing certificate sections in Employee Profile, or to curricula in LMS?

The Certificates feature is currently standalone and does not integrate with existing profile background elements or Learning currently. This is a roadmap item.

2. Can certificates from Employee Profile be migrated automatically?

This is being considered for future enhancement.

3. Can certificates already stored in employee documents be imported automatically?

Currently, the Certificates feature operates independently and does not integrate with Employee Profile background elements or Learning.

Integrations

1. Is integration with Learning available?

Considered for future roadmap.

2. Can third-party vendors automatically fetch certificates?

Not currently supported.

3. Can certificates from TIH be stored in external document management systems?

Currently out of scope

Reporting & Analytics

1.Is reporting available for certificates?

The Talent Intelligence Hub schema available for Story Reports now includes the new Certificate attribute. For additional details please refer to Available Data (Schema) in Story Report | SAP Help

 2. Is integration with Learning Analytics dashboards planned?

No current plans have been confirmed.

Access & User Scope

1. Are certificates available to external users or onboardees?

Not currently supported.

2. Are certificate workflows available without an Employee Central license?

Yes. Certificate workflows are based on MDF workflows, which are part of the foundational platform services and do not depend on an Employee Central license.

Key resources

Certificates | What’s New Viewer

Certificates | SAP Help Portal

Also you can automate goods issue withe trucks departure in freight order config to eliminate steps.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: Very interesting @MatthiasL . No HANA DB patch updates?? Wow!! It is weird to even call SAP EC Payroll system as cloud. It doesnt fit any definition of the cloud other than being in a network and being charged a subscription fee. It is just an ECC system sitting in a SAP data center (and that too not in the same data center as your SF data centers but separate). Also, the EC Payroll code base has not been optimized to leverage the capability of HANA DB; no actual benefit out of using HANA DB which is probably the reason they haven't bothered to migrate to HANA DB.

entry.title: SQL Anywhere on Linux
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

I have installed SQL Anywhere 17 full on my Windows box.

 

Now yesterday I downloaded the SQL Anywhere client on one of my Linux boxes and successfully installed it.

I already have unixODBC set up on that box and so I tried to set up a connection and then tried to run my program.

 

I used 64 bit driver as my program is compiled as 64 bit.

 

Unfortunately the program crashed inside SQLAllocHandle() call of the driver.

 

I will not have access to this machine until Friday, but does this looks familiar to anybody?

How do I set up the unixODBC with SQLAnywhere to get a successful connection?

 

I read the docs and gather that the server comes with its own Driver Manager implementation, but I'm using only the client.

 

Thank you.

 

P.S.: This is the code I'm using:

 

[code]

int ODBCDatabase::Connect(const std::wstring &selectedDSN, std::vector<std::wstring> &dbList, std::vector<std::wstring> &errorMsg)
{
int result = 0, bufferSize = 1024;
std::vector<SQLWCHAR *> errorMessage;
SQLWCHAR connectStrIn[sizeof(SQLWCHAR) * 255], driver[1024], dsn[1024], dbType[1024];
SQLSMALLINT OutConnStrLen;
SQLRETURN ret;
SQLUSMALLINT options;
SQLWCHAR *user = nullptr, *password = nullptr;
SQLWCHAR driverName[1024];
SQLWCHAR dbName[1024];
SQLWCHAR userName[1024];
std::wstring connectingDSN, connectingUser = L"", connectingPassword = L"";
pimpl.m_type = L"ODBC";
pimpl.m_pgLogFile = L"";
if( !odbc_pimpl )
odbc_pimpl = new ODBCImpl;
std::wstring::size_type pos = selectedDSN.find( L';' );
if( pos == std::wstring::npos )
connectingDSN = selectedDSN;
else
{
std::wstring temp = selectedDSN.substr( selectedDSN.find( L"DSN=" ) + 4 );
connectingDSN = temp.substr( 0, temp.find( L";") );
// connectingDSN = selectedDSN.substr( 0, pos );
temp = temp.substr( temp.find( L"UID=" ) + 4 );
connectingUser = temp.substr( 0, temp.find( L";" ) );
temp = temp.substr( temp.find( L"PWD=" ) + 4 );
connectingPassword = temp.substr( 0, temp.find( L";" ) );
user = new SQLWCHAR[connectingUser.length() + 2];
password = new SQLWCHAR[connectingPassword.length() + 2];
memset( user, '\0', connectingUser.length() + 2 );
memset( password, '\0', connectingPassword.length() + 2 );
uc_to_str_cpy( user, connectingUser );
uc_to_str_cpy( password, connectingPassword );
}
m_connectString = new SQLWCHAR[sizeof(SQLWCHAR) * 1024];
memset( dsn, 0, sizeof( dsn ) );
memset( connectStrIn, 0, sizeof( connectStrIn ) );
memset( driver, 0, sizeof( driver ) );
uc_to_str_cpy( dsn, connectingDSN.c_str() );
ret = SQLAllocHandle( SQL_HANDLE_ENV, SQL_NULL_HENV, &m_env );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
errorMsg.push_back( L"Failed to allocate memory for the connection" );
return 1;
}
ret = SQLSetEnvAttr( m_env, SQL_ATTR_ODBC_VERSION, (SQLPOINTER) SQL_OV_ODBC3, SQL_IS_INTEGER );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, ENV_ERROR );
result = 1;
}
else
{
ret = SQLAllocHandle( SQL_HANDLE_DBC, m_env, &m_hdbc );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, ENV_ERROR );
return 1;
}
else
{
if( !GetDriverForDSN( dsn, driver, errorMsg ) )
{
std::unique_ptr<SQLWCHAR[]> tempPostgres( new SQLWCHAR[13] );
memset( tempPostgres.get(), '\0', 13 );
uc_to_str_cpy( tempPostgres.get(), L"PostgreSQL " );
if( connectStrIn[0] == '\0' )
{
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L"DSN=" );
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, connectingDSN.c_str() );
}
if( equal( tempPostgres.get(), driver ) )
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";UseServerSidePrepare=1;ShowSystemTables=1;" );
std::wstring tempmySQL;
str_to_uc_cpy( tempmySQL, driver );
if( tempmySQL.find( L"myodbc" ) != std::wstring::npos )
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";NO_SCHEMA=0;NO_CATALOG=0" );
if( user && password )
{
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";UID=" );
copy_uc_to_uc( connectStrIn, user );
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";PWD=" );
copy_uc_to_uc( connectStrIn, password );
}
delete[] user;
user = nullptr;
delete[] password;
password = nullptr;
ret = SQLSetConnectAttr( m_hdbc, SQL_LOGIN_TIMEOUT, (SQLPOINTER)50, 0 );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO && ret != SQL_NO_DATA )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, 2 );
result = 1;
}
if( !result )
{
#ifdef _WIN32
options = m_ask ? SQL_DRIVER_COMPLETE : SQL_DRIVER_NOPROMPT;
#else
options = SQL_DRIVER_NOPROMPT;
#endif
ret = SQLDriverConnect( m_hdbc, m_handle, connectStrIn, SQL_NTS, m_connectString, 1024, &OutConnStrLen, options );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO && ret != SQL_NO_DATA )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, CONN_ERROR );
result = 1;
}
if( ret == SQL_NO_DATA )
{
errorMsg.push_back( L"Connection cancelled" );
return 0;
}
}

[/code]

entry.title: How to Understand SAP Green Ledger in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Why SAP Green Ledger Matters

Sustainability and financial data are often managed separately and making it difficult to align environmental impact with business performance.

SAP Green Ledger bridges this gap by integrating carbon data directly into financial accounting processes within SAP S/4HANA. This allows organizations to combine financial and environmental insights in one unified view.

With SAP Green Ledger, businesses can:

  • Align carbon emissions with financial data
  • Increase transparency for reporting and decision-making
  • Support carbon accounting alongside financial accounting
  • Enable better planning and analysis of sustainability performance

What You’ll Learn in This Course

This short, 45-minute course introduces the fundamentals of SAP Green Ledger:

  1. Financial Integration
    Understand how SAP Green Ledger relates to and integrates with financial accounting in SAP S/4HANA.
  2. Key Features and Capabilities
    Explore core functionalities, including integration, analytics, and planning capabilities.
  3. Business Value
    Learn how combining financial and sustainability data supports more informed and strategic decision-making.

Who Should Take the Course?

This course is ideal for:

  • Business Users working with finance and sustainability data
  • Consultants supporting SAP S/4HANA and sustainability solutions

No prerequisites are required, making it a great starting point for beginners.

Conclusion

Bridge the gap between sustainability and finance and explore the course “Outlining Green Ledger in SAP S/4HANA” here.
